data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze code coverage and risk hotspots for the project. They haven't run any coverage yet, so I need to first understand the project structure, then figure out how to run tests and coverage, and finally analyze the results.

Let me start by exploring the project structure.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with code coverage collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with code coverage collection

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture. The project is a .NET 9 solution with:
- `ContosoUniversity` - main project with `StudentService.cs` containing 4 methods: `Enroll`, `CalculateGpa`, `FindById`, `Search`
- `ContosoUniversity.UnitTests` - test project with MSTest and coverlet.collector

The test project already has `coverlet.collector` which can generate coverage reports. Let me run the tests with coverage collection and then analyze the results.

Let me run `dotnet test` with coverage collection using coverlet.

arguments.description: Run tests with code coverage collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-00d047705e9149ceaebd00590d281427 && dotnet test --collect:\"XPlat Code Coverage\" --results-directory ./coverage-results 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with code coverage collection", "initial_wait": 60}
